content_en:
Tawakkul — usually translated as reliance upon or trust in Allah — is one of the highest stations of the believer's spiritual journey and one of the most frequently mentioned qualities of the truly faithful in the Quran. It is not passive resignation, philosophical fatalism, or the abandonment of effort, but rather an active spiritual orientation in which the believer takes all available and appropriate means while directing the heart's deepest confidence entirely toward Allah as the ultimate cause of outcomes.

Ibn Taymiyyah's explanation of tawakkul begins with clarifying the most common misunderstanding: that trusting Allah means not working or not planning. He draws on the famous hadith in which someone asked the Prophet whether he should tie his camel or trust in Allah, and the Prophet replied: 'Tie it and trust in Allah.' This response captures the entire Islamic understanding of tawakkul: the effort is the means; tawakkul is the orientation of the heart that accompanies the effort. Abandoning the means in the name of 'tawakkul' is not higher spirituality but a form of testing Allah.

The Quran repeatedly links tawakkul to Tawhid. Those who truly trust in Allah are described as those who 'rely upon Allah and upon Allah let the believers rely' — the exclusive quality of their reliance indicates that their hearts do not simultaneously seek security from other than Allah. This does not mean ignoring practical means, but it means that the heart's deepest security, hope, and expectation are anchored in Allah rather than in plans, wealth, connections, or human power.

In the context of relief from distress, tawakkul operates as follows: the distressed believer exhausts the legitimate means available to them, then surrenders the outcome to Allah with genuine conviction that Allah's decree is the best of all possible outcomes — even when it is not the outcome one desired. This surrender is not defeated passivity but an active affirmation that the Creator who knows all things, controls all things, and whose mercy encompasses all things, is making a decision for the servant that the servant's limited vision cannot fully appreciate.

Ibn Taymiyyah quotes the statement of Ibrahim's people when they cast him into the fire: he said 'hasbiyallah wa ni'ma al-wakil' — 'Allah is sufficient for me, and He is the best disposer of affairs.' Allah then commanded the fire to be cool and safe for Ibrahim. This is the Quran's most dramatic illustration of tawakkul's reward: when the heart is fully directed toward Allah even in the most terrifying circumstances, Allah's intervention comes from directions the servant could never have anticipated. The fire that should have consumed became the safety that preserved — because the Lord of the fire is the same Lord whose servant trusted Him completely.
content_id:
Tawakal — biasanya diterjemahkan sebagai bergantung atau kepercayaan kepada Allah — adalah salah satu maqam tertinggi dalam perjalanan spiritual orang beriman dan salah satu kualitas yang paling sering disebutkan dari mereka yang benar-benar beriman dalam Al-Qur'an. Ini bukan penyerahan diri yang pasif, fatalisme filosofis, atau pengabaian usaha, melainkan sebuah orientasi spiritual yang aktif di mana orang beriman mengambil semua sarana yang tersedia dan tepat sambil mengarahkan kepercayaan terdalam hati sepenuhnya kepada Allah sebagai penyebab utama dari segala hasil.

Penjelasan Ibnu Taimiyyah tentang tawakal dimulai dengan mengklarifikasi kesalahpahaman yang paling umum: bahwa mempercayai Allah berarti tidak bekerja atau tidak merencanakan. Ia mengambil dari hadis terkenal di mana seseorang bertanya kepada Nabi apakah ia harus mengikat untanya atau bertawakal kepada Allah, dan Nabi menjawab: Ikat dan bertawakallah kepada Allah. Respons ini menangkap seluruh pemahaman Islam tentang tawakal: usaha adalah sarana; tawakal adalah orientasi hati yang menyertai usaha. Meninggalkan sarana atas nama tawakal bukanlah spiritualitas yang lebih tinggi tetapi suatu bentuk menguji Allah.

Al-Qur'an secara berulang menghubungkan tawakal dengan tauhid. Mereka yang benar-benar bertawakal kepada Allah digambarkan sebagai mereka yang bertawakal kepada Allah dan hanya kepada Allah hendaknya orang-orang beriman bertawakal — kualitas eksklusif dari ketergantungan mereka menunjukkan bahwa hati mereka tidak secara bersamaan mencari keamanan dari selain Allah. Ini tidak berarti mengabaikan sarana-sarana praktis, tetapi berarti bahwa keamanan terdalam, harapan, dan ekspektasi hati tertambat pada Allah daripada pada rencana, kekayaan, koneksi, atau kekuatan manusia.

Dalam konteks keringanan dari kesulitan, tawakal beroperasi sebagai berikut: orang beriman yang dalam kesulitan menghabiskan semua sarana yang sah yang tersedia bagi mereka, kemudian menyerahkan hasilnya kepada Allah dengan keyakinan tulus bahwa ketetapan Allah adalah yang terbaik dari semua kemungkinan hasil — bahkan ketika itu bukan hasil yang diinginkan seseorang. Penyerahan diri ini bukan kepasifan yang kalah tetapi penegasan aktif bahwa Pencipta yang mengetahui segalanya, mengendalikan segalanya, dan yang rahmat-Nya meliputi segalanya, sedang membuat keputusan bagi hamba yang penglihatan terbatasnya tidak dapat sepenuhnya menghargainya.

Ibnu Taimiyyah mengutip pernyataan Ibrahim ketika kaumnya melemparkannya ke dalam api: beliau berucap Hasbiyallah wa ni'mal-wakil — Allah cukup bagiku, dan Dia adalah sebaik-baik pelindung. Allah kemudian memerintahkan api untuk menjadi dingin dan selamat bagi Ibrahim. Inilah ilustrasi Al-Qur'an yang paling dramatis tentang pahala tawakal: ketika hati sepenuhnya diarahkan kepada Allah bahkan dalam keadaan paling menakutkan, intervensi Allah datang dari arah yang tidak pernah dapat diantisipasi oleh hamba. Api yang seharusnya membakar menjadi keselamatan yang menjaga — karena Tuhan dari api adalah Tuhan yang sama yang hambanya mempercayai-Nya sepenuhnya.
content_ar: التوكل — usually translated as reliance upon or trust in الله — is one of the highest stations of the المؤمن's spiritual journey and one of the most frequently mentioned qualities of the truly faithful in the القرآن الكريم. It is not passive resignation, philosophical fatalism, or the abandonment of effort, but rather an active spiritual orientation in which the المؤمن takes all available and appropriate means بينما directing the القلب's deepest confidence entirely toward الله as the ultimate cause of outcomes. ابن تيمية's explanation of التوكل begins with clarifying the most common misunderstanding: that trusting الله means not working or not planning. He draws on the famous الحديث in which someone asked النبي ﷺ whether he should tie his camel or trust in الله, and النبي ﷺ replied: 'Tie it and trust in الله.' This response captures the entire Islamic الفهم of التوكل: the effort is the means; التوكل is the orientation of the القلب that accompanies the effort. Abandoning the means in the name of 'التوكل' is not higher spirituality but a form of الابتلاء الله. The القرآن الكريم repeatedly links التوكل to Tawhid. Those who truly trust in الله are described as those who 'rely upon الله and upon الله let the المؤمنون rely' — the exclusive quality of their reliance indicates that their القلوب do not simultaneously seek security from other than الله. This does not mean ignoring practical means, but it means that the القلب's deepest security, الأمل, and expectation are anchored in الله rather than in plans, الثروة, connections, or human power. In the context of الفرج from distress, التوكل operates as follows: the distressed المؤمن exhausts the legitimate means available to them, then surrenders the outcome to الله with genuine conviction that الله's decree is the best of all possible outcomes — even عندما it is not the outcome one desired. This surrender is not defeated passivity but an active affirmation that the Creator who knows all things, controls all things, and whose الرحمة encompasses all things, is making a decision for the servant that the servant's limited vision cannot fully appreciate. ابن تيمية quotes the statement of إبراهيم ﷺ's people عندما they cast him into the fire: قال 'hasbiyallah wa ni'ma al-wakil' — 'الله is sufficient for me, and He is the best disposer of affairs.' الله then commanded the fire to be cool and safe for إبراهيم ﷺ. This is the القرآن الكريم's most dramatic illustration of التوكل's الأجر: عندما the القلب is fully directed toward الله even in the most terrifying circumstances, الله's intervention comes from directions the servant could never have anticipated. The fire that should have consumed became the safety that preserved — لأن the Lord of the fire is the same Lord whose servant trusted Him completely.
